{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}

{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)-qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)

{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38年1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion
