A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|12=draft|12=draft}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏|12=draft|12=draft}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=draft|12=draft}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=draft|12=draft}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=draft|12=draft}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=draft|12=draft}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft|12=draft}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=draft|12=draft}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日|12=draft|12=draft}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=draft|12=draft}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|12=draft|12=draft}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|12=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=draft}}. revision.comment: 63 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:春日神社 (春日市) up to rev 102294661
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[春日市]]春日1丁目110 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[姫大神]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[神護景雲]]二年（[[768年]]） |例祭 = [[4月15日]]（春籠の祭）
[[7月15日]]（夏籠の祭）
[[10月10日]]（秋季例大祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[春日市]]にある、同市の名称の由来ともなっている[[神社]]である。 ==境内== {{右| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|「春日神祠」扁額]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|旧社格の碑]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|奥の路と社殿]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|若宮社]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|薬師堂]] }} [[天児屋根命]]、[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[姫大神]]を[[祭神]]とし、[[天手力雄命|太力雄命]]および[[太玉命]]を祭神とする末社たる若宮社、また[[薬師堂]]などを境内に有する。 入口の[[鳥居]]は[[黒田一利]]により[[宝永]]七年（[[1710年]]）に奉納されたもの（奥部のそれは[[平成]]八年（[[1996年]]）12月の奉納）。最奥部たる社殿の裏手には、[[承天寺]]の末寺であった大光寺の跡が遺され、[[十三仏]]が安置されている。 境内に生える11本の[[クスノキ|樟]]はまとめて『春日の杜』と呼ばれ、県により[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）の[[1月16日]]をもって[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社であった。[[牛頸川]]に架かる御潮井橋（おしおいばし）から直線に[[参道]]が延びている。 ==歴史== ===起源=== 発生は古くこの地に[[天智天皇|中大兄皇子]]が天児屋根命を祀ったことに始まると伝えられる。四神を祀る神社としての春日神社の起源は[[藤原田麻呂]]が大宰大弐として[[大宰府]]にいた[[神護景雲]]二年（[[768年]]）にさかのぼる。藤原家の祖神たる天児屋根命がこの地に祀られていることを知った田麻呂は、自身の故郷である[[大和国]]春日の[[春日大社]]より武甕槌命、経津主命、姫大神の三柱を[[神籬]]に迎え祀って春日大明神として社殿を創建した。 ===焼失と再建=== [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]末期の[[天正]]十四年（[[1586年]]）には、戦乱のさなかで薩摩の[[島津氏|島津]]勢の兵火に遭い、社殿、末社、宝蔵、古文書などの一切が焼失。荒廃の途を辿った社殿の再建は、[[黒田長政]]の[[家老]][[黒田一成]]の手による、[[寛永]]四年（[[1627年]]）のそれを待たなければならなかった。その後[[元禄]]九年（[[1698年]]）の改築と平成八年（[[1996年]]）の総改修を経て現在の姿に至っている。 ==行事== ;春日の婿押し（むこおし） :古来から毎年一月十四日夜に行われるところの数百年の歴史を持つと伝えられる年中行事である。春日神社の氏子のうち、前年に結婚した新郎新婦を宿（現在は敷地内に建つ公民館）で披露し（'''左義長点火'''（さきっちょうてんか）、'''宿の行事'''）、拝殿と境内で『祝い歌』を歌いながら婿を揉み（'''婿押し'''）、最後に若水を掛けて祝福する行事（'''若水祝い'''）と、五穀豊穣と開運を願っての樽せりや'''お汐井取り'''（おしおいとり）が行われ、'''千秋楽'''で幕を閉じる。農村社会の様々な伝承が複合された全国的にも珍しい貴重な民俗行事であるとされ、国指定の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に列せられている。 ==例祭== *四月第三日曜日:春籠の祭 *七月第三日曜日:夏籠の祭 *十月第三日曜日:秋季例大祭（[[宮座]]） ==末社== *三郎天神 *:境内ではなく離れにある小社で、[[ハニヤス|埴安命]](はにやすのみこと)を祀る。創建暦年不詳。春日神社の史料のひとつ・『春日大明神記録』や、[[貝原益軒]]の編纂による史書・[[筑前国続風土記]]にもその名が現れる。『[http://w.nsview.net/v/00012/ 春日三郎天神社@紀行道中写真館]』 *九郎天神 *:境内ではなく離れ、ちょうど参道と[[牛頸川]]が交差する地点にある小社で、黒男大明神（供老大明神）を祀る。創建暦年不詳。『春日大明神記録』や筑前国続風土記にもその名が現れる。『[http://w.nsview.net/v/00011/ 春日九郎天神社@紀行道中写真館]』 *地禄天神 *:三末社のうち最も離れにあるそれで、埴安命を祀る。創建暦年不詳。筑前国続風土記にわずかながらその記録が現れる。『[http://w.nsview.net/v/00024/ 春日地禄天神社@紀行道中写真館]』 ==周辺== 近場に、『上の地蔵』、『下の地蔵』などといった、数々の史跡が存在している。また社領の東脇（入口側）を南北に通る道を『春の社通り』といい、これを挟んだ東の一画に[[浄土真宗本願寺派]]の寺院たる月光山長円寺がある。 ==所在地== *福岡県春日市春日1丁目110番地に所在する。[[福岡県道31号福岡筑紫野線|県道31号線]]『春日神社入口』交差点のわずか北方。 ==交通アクセス== *[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[鹿児島本線]][[大野城駅]]から徒歩18分（1500m）。 *JR九州鹿児島本線[[春日駅 (福岡県)|春日駅]]もしくは大野城駅から[[やよい]]春日原線に乗車し「春日神社前」下車。 *[[西鉄天神大牟田線]][[春日原駅]]から[[西鉄バス二日市|西鉄バス]]6系統（春日経由月の浦営業所ゆき）に乗車し「春日」下車。 *マイカーの場合、[[九州自動車道]][[太宰府インターチェンジ]]から2.8㎞。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja, Kasuga}} * {{Official website|name=春日神社ホームページ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや}} [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:春日市の建築物]] [[Category:春日市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[春日神社 (春日市)]] to [[Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 春日神社 |画像 = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[春日市]]春日1丁目110 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天児屋根命]]
[[武甕槌命]]
[[経津主命]]
[[姫大神]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[神護景雲]]二年（[[768年]]） |例祭 = [[4月15日]]（春籠の祭）
[[7月15日]]（夏籠の祭）
[[10月10日]]（秋季例大祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=春日神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[春日市]]にある、同市の名称の由来ともなっている[[神社]]である。 ==境内== {{右| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|「春日神祠」扁額]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|旧社格の碑]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|奥の路と社殿]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|若宮社]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|薬師堂]] }} [[天児屋根命]]、[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[姫大神]]を[[祭神]]とし、[[天手力雄命|太力雄命]]および[[太玉命]]を祭神とする末社たる若宮社、また[[薬師堂]]などを境内に有する。 入口の[[鳥居]]は[[黒田一利]]により[[宝永]]七年（[[1710年]]）に奉納されたもの（奥部のそれは[[平成]]八年（[[1996年]]）12月の奉納）。最奥部たる社殿の裏手には、[[承天寺]]の末寺であった大光寺の跡が遺され、[[十三仏]]が安置されている。 境内に生える11本の[[クスノキ|樟]]はまとめて『春日の杜』と呼ばれ、県により[[昭和]]38年（[[1963年]]）の[[1月16日]]をもって[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社であった。[[牛頸川]]に架かる御潮井橋（おしおいばし）から直線に[[参道]]が延びている。 ==歴史== ===起源=== 発生は古くこの地に[[天智天皇|中大兄皇子]]が天児屋根命を祀ったことに始まると伝えられる。四神を祀る神社としての春日神社の起源は[[藤原田麻呂]]が大宰大弐として[[大宰府]]にいた[[神護景雲]]二年（[[768年]]）にさかのぼる。藤原家の祖神たる天児屋根命がこの地に祀られていることを知った田麻呂は、自身の故郷である[[大和国]]春日の[[春日大社]]より武甕槌命、経津主命、姫大神の三柱を[[神籬]]に迎え祀って春日大明神として社殿を創建した。 ===焼失と再建=== [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]末期の[[天正]]十四年（[[1586年]]）には、戦乱のさなかで薩摩の[[島津氏|島津]]勢の兵火に遭い、社殿、末社、宝蔵、古文書などの一切が焼失。荒廃の途を辿った社殿の再建は、[[黒田長政]]の[[家老]][[黒田一成]]の手による、[[寛永]]四年（[[1627年]]）のそれを待たなければならなかった。その後[[元禄]]九年（[[1698年]]）の改築と平成八年（[[1996年]]）の総改修を経て現在の姿に至っている。 ==行事== ;春日の婿押し（むこおし） :古来から毎年一月十四日夜に行われるところの数百年の歴史を持つと伝えられる年中行事である。春日神社の氏子のうち、前年に結婚した新郎新婦を宿（現在は敷地内に建つ公民館）で披露し（'''左義長点火'''（さきっちょうてんか）、'''宿の行事'''）、拝殿と境内で『祝い歌』を歌いながら婿を揉み（'''婿押し'''）、最後に若水を掛けて祝福する行事（'''若水祝い'''）と、五穀豊穣と開運を願っての樽せりや'''お汐井取り'''（おしおいとり）が行われ、'''千秋楽'''で幕を閉じる。農村社会の様々な伝承が複合された全国的にも珍しい貴重な民俗行事であるとされ、国指定の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に列せられている。 ==例祭== *四月第三日曜日:春籠の祭 *七月第三日曜日:夏籠の祭 *十月第三日曜日:秋季例大祭（[[宮座]]） ==末社== *三郎天神 *:境内ではなく離れにある小社で、[[ハニヤス|埴安命]](はにやすのみこと)を祀る。創建暦年不詳。春日神社の史料のひとつ・『春日大明神記録』や、[[貝原益軒]]の編纂による史書・[[筑前国続風土記]]にもその名が現れる。『[http://w.nsview.net/v/00012/ 春日三郎天神社@紀行道中写真館]』 *九郎天神 *:境内ではなく離れ、ちょうど参道と[[牛頸川]]が交差する地点にある小社で、黒男大明神（供老大明神）を祀る。創建暦年不詳。『春日大明神記録』や筑前国続風土記にもその名が現れる。『[http://w.nsview.net/v/00011/ 春日九郎天神社@紀行道中写真館]』 *地禄天神 *:三末社のうち最も離れにあるそれで、埴安命を祀る。創建暦年不詳。筑前国続風土記にわずかながらその記録が現れる。『[http://w.nsview.net/v/00024/ 春日地禄天神社@紀行道中写真館]』 ==周辺== 近場に、『上の地蔵』、『下の地蔵』などといった、数々の史跡が存在している。また社領の東脇（入口側）を南北に通る道を『春の社通り』といい、これを挟んだ東の一画に[[浄土真宗本願寺派]]の寺院たる月光山長円寺がある。 ==所在地== *福岡県春日市春日1丁目110番地に所在する。[[福岡県道31号福岡筑紫野線|県道31号線]]『春日神社入口』交差点のわずか北方。 ==交通アクセス== *[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[鹿児島本線]][[大野城駅]]から徒歩18分（1500m）。 *JR九州鹿児島本線[[春日駅 (福岡県)|春日駅]]もしくは大野城駅から[[やよい]]春日原線に乗車し「春日神社前」下車。 *[[西鉄天神大牟田線]][[春日原駅]]から[[西鉄バス二日市|西鉄バス]]6系統（春日経由月の浦営業所ゆき）に乗車し「春日」下車。 *マイカーの場合、[[九州自動車道]][[太宰府インターチェンジ]]から2.8㎞。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja, Kasuga}} * {{Official website|name=春日神社ホームページ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}

It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=draft}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=draft}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂|12=draft}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|12=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=draft}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|12=draft}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺|12=draft}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏|12=draft}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物|12=draft}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=draft}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=draft}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=draft}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=draft}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=draft}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|12=draft}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|12=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=draft}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|12=draft}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日|12=draft}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=draft}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=draft}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=draft}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=draft}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=draft}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=draft}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|12=draft}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|12=draft}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|12=draft}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}

{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 15|qq=}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 15|qq=}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 10|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-dō|qq=}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1710|qq=}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōten-ji|qq=}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Buddhas|qq=}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monument of Japan|qq=}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1586|qq=}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu clan|qq=}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1627|qq=}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagamasa|qq=}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1698|qq=}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14|qq=}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Jō Station|qq=}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|qq=}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

{{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Jō Station|qq=}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|qq=}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|ja|春日市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga, Fukuoka|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|ja|4月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 15|qq=}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|ja|7月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 15|qq=}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 10|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kasuga|ja|春日市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, along with a {{ill|Yakushi-dō|ja|薬師堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-dō|qq=}}. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1710|qq=}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|ja|承天寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōten-ji|qq=}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|ja|十三仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Buddhas|qq=}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Japan|ja|天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monument of Japan|qq=}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}}—from {{ill|Kasuga Taisha|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-taisha|qq=}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1586|qq=}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu clan|qq=}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1627|qq=}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagamasa|qq=}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1698|qq=}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|ja|1月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14|qq=}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=}}, a nearby {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗本願寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōno Jō Station|ja|大野城駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Jō Station|qq=}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|ja|春日駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|qq=}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]]

It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1710|qq=}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1586|qq=}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1627|qq=}} by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1698|qq=}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:春日市の建築物
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=}} | festivals = [[:en:April 15|April 15]] (Spring Festival)
[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1710|qq=}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|ja|天正十四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1586|qq=}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1627|qq=}} by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1698|qq=}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:春日市の建築物]]
