[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1710|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In [[:en:1586|1586]], the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|ja|寛永四年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1627|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|ja|元禄九年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1698|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:春日市の建築物]] [[Category:春日市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]]

{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | festivals = [[:en:April 15|April 15]] (Spring Festival)
[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|ja|宝永七年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1710|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In [[:en:1586|1586]], the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1627|1627]] by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in [[:en:1698|1698]] and {{ill|1996|ja|平成八年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1996|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:春日市の建築物]] [[Category:春日市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]]

From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | festivals = [[:en:April 15|April 15]] (Spring Festival)
[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in [[:en:1710|1710]] by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in [[:en:1996|1996]]. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In {{ill|768|ja|神護景雲二年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In [[:en:1586|1586]], the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1627|1627]] by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in [[:en:1698|1698]] and [[:en:1996|1996]]. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:春日市の建築物]] [[Category:春日市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]]

From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| revision.comment: Text replacement - "市の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:768|768]] | festivals = [[:en:April 15|April 15]] (Spring Festival)
[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in [[:en:1710|1710]] by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in [[:en:1996|1996]]. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In [[:en:768|768]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In [[:en:1586|1586]], the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1627|1627]] by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in [[:en:1698|1698]] and [[:en:1996|1996]]. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:春日市の建築物]] [[Category:春日の Municipal History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]]

Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:768|768]] | festivals = [[:en:April 15|April 15]] (Spring Festival)
[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in [[:en:1710|1710]] by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in [[:en:1996|1996]]. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In [[:en:768|768]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In [[:en:1586|1586]], the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1627|1627]] by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in [[:en:1698|1698]] and [[:en:1996|1996]]. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasuga City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasuga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]]

Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11513666]] Wikidata name: Kasuga Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11513666]] Wikidata name: Kasuga Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) | location = [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:768|768]] | festivals = [[:en:April 15|April 15]] (Spring Festival)
[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in [[:en:1710|1710]] by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in [[:en:1996|1996]]. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In [[:en:768|768]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In [[:en:1586|1586]], the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1627|1627]] by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in [[:en:1698|1698]] and [[:en:1996|1996]]. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasuga City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasuga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]]

to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11513666]] Wikidata name: Kasuga Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11513666]] Wikidata name: Kasuga Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg | location = [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.52188888888889|N|130.4692777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:768|768]] | festivals = [[:en:April 15|April 15]] (Spring Festival)
[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Summer Festival)
[[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture= {{ill|Kasuga|qid=Q47084}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kasuga, Fukuoka|Kasuga]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in [[:en:1710|1710]] by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|qq=}}. A newer torii gate was added in [[:en:1996|1996]]. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of [[:en:Jōten-ji|Jōten-ji]], which houses [[:en:Thirteen Buddhas|Thirteen Buddhas]]. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument of Japan]] on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]. In [[:en:768|768]], {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|qq=}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]]s to 比売神}}—from [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]] in the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In [[:en:1586|1586]], the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]]. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1627|1627]] by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=}}. Subsequent renovations took place in [[:en:1698|1698]] and [[:en:1996|1996]]. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:1627|1627]] by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|qq=|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in [[:en:1698|1698]] and [[:en:1996|1996]]. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|qq=|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|qq=|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|qq=|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby [[:en:Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|Jōdo Shinshū]] temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from [[:en:Ōnojō Station|Ōno Jō Station]] (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From [[:en:Kasuga Station|Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)]] or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|qq=|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Kasuga)]] [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:Architecture of Kasuga City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasuga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka
