[[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11513666]] Wikidata name: Kasuga Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11513666]] Wikidata name: Kasuga Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.52188888888889|N|130.4692777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture= {{ill|Kasuga|WD=Q47084}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki s to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|1=Kuroda Ichitoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60613431}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument of Japan}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] {{wikidata link|Q11513666}} Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] {{wikidata link|Q11513666}} revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.52188888888889|N|130.4692777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture= {{ill|Kasuga|WD=Q47084}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki s to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|1=Kuroda Ichitoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60613431}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument of Japan}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] {{wikidata link|Q11513666}} The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|1=Kuroda Ichitoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60613431}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument of Japan}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] {{wikidata link|Q11513666}} [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.52188888888889|N|130.4692777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture= {{ill|Kasuga|WD=Q47084}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki s to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|1=Kuroda Ichitoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60613431}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument of Japan}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Miyaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen'nokuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture= {{ill|Kasuga|WD=Q47084|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki s to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|1=Q60613431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60613431}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji|1=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas|1=Thirteen Buddhas}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument of Japan|1=Natural monument}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586|1=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan|1=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627|1=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698|1=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14|1=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Q11454763|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū|1=Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station|1=Ōnojō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|1=Kasuga Station}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga, Fukuoka|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.52188888888889|N|130.4692777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture= {{ill|Kasuga|WD=Q47084|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki s to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|1=Q60613431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60613431}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji|1=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas|1=Thirteen Buddhas}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument of Japan|1=Natural monument}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586|1=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan|1=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627|1=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698|1=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14|1=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Q11454763|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū|1=Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station|1=Ōnojō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|1=Kasuga Station}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] {{wikidata A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji|1=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas|1=Thirteen Buddhas}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586|1=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan|1=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627|1=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698|1=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14|1=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Q11454763|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū|1=Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station|1=Ōnojō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|1=Kasuga Station}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga Shrine] {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (春日市)|version=102294661|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Commons category|Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)}} [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]] {{wikidata link|Q11513666}} [[Category:Architecture of Kasuga City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga Redirects]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kasuga]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kasuga-jinja (Kasuga, Fukuoka)]][[de:Kasuga-Schrein]][[ja:春日神社 (春日市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasuga Shrine (春日神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Haiden and Honden of Kasuga Shrine in Kasuga, Fukuoka.jpg | location = {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga, Fukuoka|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.52188888888889|N|130.4692777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}
{{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki s to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}} | festivals = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} (Summer Festival)
{{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} (Autumn Grand Festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kasuga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture= {{ill|Kasuga|WD=Q47084|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}} }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kasuga, Fukuoka|en|Kasuga, Fukuoka|WD=Q47084|lt=Kasuga|1=Kasuga, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is notable as the origin of the city's name. == Grounds == {{right| [[File:Kasuga shrine 200608022110.JPG|none|205px|thumb|“Kasuga Shrine” plaque]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607042230.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Former shrine Rank monument]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200608022040.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Pathway and shrine building]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607292025.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Wakamiya Shrine]] [[File:Kasuga shrine Up200607041940.JPG|none|thumb|205px|Yakushidō]] }} The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}} as its primary deities. It also features the Wakamiya Shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|ja|天手力雄命|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Torifune no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki s to アメノタヂカラオ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}} and {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki s to フトダマ}}, along with a [[:en:Yaoshi Dian|Yakushi-dō]]. The main torii gate was donated in {{ill|1710|en|1710|WD=Q6861|lt=1710|1=1710}} by {{ill|Kuroda Ichitoshi|ja|黒田一利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ichitoshi|1=Q60613431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60613431}}. A newer torii gate was added in {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. Behind the main shrine building is the site of Daikōji, a former sub-temple of {{ill|Jōten-ji|en|Jōten-ji|WD=Q3191242|lt=Jōten-ji|1=Jōten-ji}}, which houses {{ill|Thirteen Buddhas|en|Thirteen Buddhas|WD=Q3538361|lt=Thirteen Buddhas|1=Thirteen Buddhas}}. Eleven camphor trees on the shrine grounds, collectively known as “Kasuga no Mori,” were designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument of Japan|1=Natural monument}} on {{ill|January 16, 1963|ja|昭和38th year 1月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 16, 1963|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A straight path leads from the {{ill|Oshioibashi Bridge|ja|御潮井橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshioibashi Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, crossing the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}} to the shrine. == History == === Origin === The shrine traces its origins to the enshrinement of {{ill|Amenokoyane no Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokoyane no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki s to 天児屋命}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}. In {{ill|768|en|768|WD=Q29961|lt=768|1=768}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamaro|ja|藤原田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamaro|1=Fujiwara no Tamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623405}}, then Governor of Dazaifu, brought three deities—{{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki s to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki s to 経津主神}}, and {{ill|Hime no Kami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki s to 比売神|1=Himegami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22070227}}—from {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and established the shrine as “Kasuga Daimyōjin.” === Destruction and Reconstruction === In {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586|1=1586}}, the shrine was destroyed during an attack by the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan|1=Shimazu clan}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|1627|en|1627|WD=Q6833|lt=1627|1=1627}} by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}’s retainer, {{ill|Kuroda Kazunari|ja|黒田一成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Kazunari|1=Kuroda Kazushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678557}}. Subsequent renovations took place in {{ill|1698|en|1698|WD=Q7706|lt=1698|1=1698}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. == Rituals and Festivals == ;Kasuga’s Muko-oshi (Groom Pushing) : Held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14|1=January 14}}, this ritual involves a celebration of newlyweds and traditional ceremonies such as the Sagi-cho Bonfire, Groom Pushing, and the Wakamiya Water Blessing. Designated a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}, it combines agricultural and community traditions. === festivals === * Third Sunday of April: Spring Festival * Third Sunday of July: Summer Festival * Third Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|Miyaza|ja|宮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaza|1=Q11454763|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454763}}) == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Saburō Tenjin Shrine''': Dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its history is noted in the {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja|春日大明神記録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikuzen Province Fudoki|ja|筑前国続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province Fudoki|1=Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108571667}}. * '''Kurō Tenjin Shrine''': Located near the {{ill|Ushikubi River|ja|牛頸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushikubi River|1=Ushikubi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570251}}, it enshrines Kuroo Daimyōjin. * '''Chiroku Tenjin Shrine''': The farthest subsidiary shrine, also dedicated to {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|埴安命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Nearby == Close to the shrine are various historic landmarks, including “Upper Jizō” and “Lower Jizō.” The road east of the shrine, called “Haru no Yashiro-dōri,” leads to {{ill|Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja|月光山長円寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekkōzan Chōen-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a nearby {{ill|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|en|Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha|WD=Q10389102|lt=Jōdo Shinshū|1=Jodo Shinshu Hongwanji-ha}} temple. == Address == * 1-110 Kasuga, Kasuga City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan == Access == * '''By Foot''': 18 minutes from {{ill|Ōnojō Station|en|Ōnojō Station|WD=Q5359644|lt=Ōno Jō Station|1=Ōnojō Station}} (JR Kyushu Kagoshima Main Line). * '''By Bus''': From {{ill|Kasuga Station|en|Kasuga Station|WD=Q843055|lt=Kasuga Station (Fukuoka)|1=Kasuga Station}} or Ōno Jō Station, take the Yayoi Kasugahara Line to “Kasuga Shrine-mae.” * '''By Car''': 2.8 km from {{ill|Dazaifu Interchange|ja|太宰府インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazaifu Interchange|1=Dazaifu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575734}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://kasugajinja.org/ Official website of Kasuga
